sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, so clicking on the yellow square didn't change anything, and pressing UP moved the light green square from bottom-center [42:43, 10:11] to middle-center [40:41, 10:11]. The yellow square stayed at [20:21, 24:25].
So the arrow keys only move the light green square, not the yellow square. The yellow square can only be moved by clicking on the right shapes.
Let me think about the game again. The right shapes have a "stepped" pattern. The left blocks rearrange when I click on the right shapes. The colored squares move when I click on the right shapes or use arrow keys.
Maybe the game is about arranging the left blocks to match the right shapes. The right shapes have a "stepped" pattern. The left blocks need to form a similar "stepped" pattern.
The current configuration is:
- Blue rect: [20:23, 18:21] (4x4) - top-right
- Red: [24:29, 18:21] (6x4) - below blue rect (vertical)
- Gray: [30:33, 8:11] (4x4) - middle-left
- Dark red: [30:33, 18:21] (4x4) - middle-right
- Checkerboard: [34:37, 8:11] (4x4) - bottom-left
- Light green: [40:41, 10:11] (middle-center)
The blue rect and red block form a vertical stack at the right. The gray and dark red blocks form a horizontal row in the middle. The checkerboard is at the bottom-left.
Hmm, let me think about the "stepped" pattern again. The right shapes have:
- Red shape: rows 17-21, cols ~42-54
  - Rows 17-19: 7 wide (centered)
  - Rows 20-21: 13 wide (centered)
- Blue shape: rows 34-38, cols ~42-54
  - Rows 34-36: 10 wide (centered)
  - Rows 37-38: 15 wide (centered)
The red shape has a "stepped" pattern that widens as it goes down. The blue shape has the same pattern.
Maybe the left blocks need to form a similar "stepped" pattern. The blocks are:
- Gray: 4x4
- Red: 4x6 (horizontal) or 6x4 (vertical)
- Dark red: 4x4
- Blue: 4x4
- Checkerboard: 4x4
To form a "stepped" pattern, I need:
- Top: narrow block (4x4) at center
- Middle: wider block (4x6 or 4x8) at center
- Bottom: widest block (4x12 or 4x14) at center
But the blocks don't add up to this pattern. The total width of the blocks is 4 + 6 + 4 + 4 + 4 = 22, which is too wide.
Wait, maybe the "stepped" pattern is not about the left blocks, but about the colored squares. The colored squares need to be positioned to match the "stepped" pattern.
Actually, let me reconsider the game. Maybe the game is about "painting" the left blocks to match the right shapes. The colored squares are "brushes" that paint the blocks. When I click on a right shape, it "paints" the corresponding left block with that shape's color.
But I haven't seen any evidence of the blocks being painted. The blocks just rearrange when I click on the right shapes.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the game is about matching the colors of the left blocks to the right shapes. The red shape is red, and the blue shape is blue. The left blocks include a red block and a blue block.
Maybe the goal is to have the red block adjacent to the red shape's position on the left side, and the blue block adjacent to the blue shape's position on the left side. The red shape is at the top-right of the right panel, and the blue shape is at the bottom-right. The red block needs to be at the top-right of the left panel, and the blue block needs to be at the bottom-left.
But the current configuration has the blue block at the top-right and the red block in the middle. The red block is not at the top-right.
Wait, actually the current configuration has:
- Blue rect: [20:23, 18:21] (4x4) - top-right
- Red: [24:29, 18:21] (6x4) - below blue rect (vertical)
The red block is below the blue rect, forming a vertical stack. This doesn't match the "stepped" pattern.
Let me try clicking on the blue shape and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the blue shape to see what happens
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click on blue shape to rearrange left blocks')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 35, 'col': 48}])

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Left blocks after clicking blue ===")
for r in range(18, 44):
    line = ascii_lines[r][7:22]
    if any(c not in 'c' for c in line):
        print(f"  {r}: {line}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        r0, r1 = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
        c0, c1 = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"\nLight green: [{r0}:{r1},{c0}:{c1}]")
    if n['color'] == 'Y':
        b = n['boundary']
        r0, r1 = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
        c0, c1 = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Yellow: [{r0}:{r1},{c0}:{c1}]")

print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
